title: eleocharis flavescens var. dussiana
intro: Eleocharis flavescens var. dussiana, also known as Duss's Spike-rush or Spike-rush, is a type of flowering plant in the sedge family Cyperaceae, commonly found in wetlands and aquatic environments. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of essential oils and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Eleocharis flavescens var. dussiana include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and expectorant. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically harvested, dried, and used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it can also be used as a tea or added to ointments and salves for topical application.
